task_completion.description: Agent opened draw.io to create the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io (diagrams.net) application interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Agent created a hierarchical tree structure with Selector and Sequence nodes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tree diagram is visible with multiple connected nodes (at least 15). Some nodes should be explicitly labeled 'Selector' or 'Sequence'.

task_completion.description: Agent implemented the four priority behaviors (Self-Preservation, Combat, Investigate, Idle).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Nodes contain specific text from the logic spec, such as 'Health', '20%', 'Player Visible', 'Bite', 'Spit', 'Noise', 'Idle', and 'Wander'.

task_completion.description: Agent applied the correct color styling to the nodes according to the style guide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Control nodes (Selector/Sequence) are colored light grey (#f5f5f5), Condition nodes (e.g., Health < 20%, Player Visible) are light yellow (#fff2cc), and Action nodes (e.g., Bite, Wander) are light blue (#dae8fc).

task_completion.description: Agent saved the diagram as a .drawio file in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows 'zombie_behavior_tree.drawio' being saved to the '~/Diagrams/' folder, or the draw.io tab title reflects this filename.

task_completion.description: Agent exported the diagram as a PNG file to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager shows 'zombie_behavior_tree.png' being saved to the '~/Diagrams/exports/' folder.

integrity.description: Agent used the draw.io graphical interface to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent dragging and dropping nodes, connecting them, and typing text in the draw.io UI, rather than generating an XML file via terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent used the draw.io export functionality to create the PNG.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the draw.io menu (e.g., File -> Export as -> PNG) rather than using a command-line conversion tool or renaming a file extension.

integrity.description: Agent manually applied styles using the draw.io interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the format panel on the right side of draw.io to change fill colors to the specified hex codes or standard palette colors.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The behavior tree must contain at least 15 nodes. Required text elements to look for in the diagram include: 'Health', '20%', 'Selector', 'Sequence', 'Player Visible', 'Bite', 'Spit', 'Noise', 'Idle', and 'Wander'. The required colors are: Condition nodes = Light Yellow (#fff2cc), Action nodes = Light Blue (#dae8fc), Control nodes = Light Grey (#f5f5f5). The final files must be named 'zombie_behavior_tree.drawio' (saved in ~/Diagrams/) and 'zombie_behavior_tree.png' (exported to ~/Diagrams/exports/).
